query: Analyze a high-aspect-ratio wing designed for low-speed soaring: span 20 m, root chord 1.2 m, twist_cp=[0.0, -2.0, -4.0] (3 CPs, washout toward tip), chord_cp=[1.0, 0.9, 0.7] (3 CPs, tapered chord), sweep 5 deg, dihedral 5 deg, t/c 0.15. Viscous drag, no wave drag. Mach 0.1 and 0.2, alpha -5 to 20 deg, rho=1.225 kg/m3. Plot L/D vs alpha and drag polars.

Minimize CD of a rectangular wing with span 20 m, root chord 3 m at Mach 0.5 sea level. Design variables: alpha (-10 to 10 deg), taper (0.2 to 1.0), sweep (-5 to 35 deg). Constraints: CL = 0.55. Report optimal sweep and taper.

Two-point drag minimization on a CRM wing. Point 0: Mach 0.84, rho=0.38, CL=0.44. Point 1: Mach 0.84, rho=0.38, CL=0.54. DVs: alpha per point (-15 to 15 deg), twist_cp (5 CPs, -5 to 8 deg). Objective: minimize sum of CD across both points.

Aerostructural optimization of a CRM wing with tube spar (E=70 GPa, G=30 GPa, yield=500 MPa, safety=2.5). Cruise: Mach 0.84, rho=0.38 kg/m3, W0=100000 kg, range 9000 km, CT=17e-6/s. DVs: alpha, twist (5 CPs), tube thickness (3 CPs, 0.005 to 0.3 m). Minimize fuelburn. Constraints: no structural failure, lift equals weight.

Minimize fuel burn of a uCRM wingbox wing at Mach 0.85, rho=0.348 kg/m3. Use aluminum (E=73.1 GPa, G=27.5 GPa, yield=420 MPa, safety=1.5, mrho=2780 kg/m3). W0=128000 kg, reserve fuel 15000 kg, range 14307 km, CT=0.53/3600/s. DVs: twist (4 CPs, -15 to 15 deg), spar thickness (4 CPs, 0.003 to 0.1 m), skin thickness (4 CPs, 0.003 to 0.1 m), t/c (4 CPs, 0.07 to 0.2), alpha_maneuver, fuel_mass. Constraints: CL=0.5 at cruise, structural failure <= 0 at maneuver (load factor 2.5), fuel volume delta >= 0.

Minimize the structural mass of a CRM wing tube spar under a uniform nodal load of 2e5 N at all nodes. Material: E=70 GPa, G=30 GPa, yield=500 MPa, safety factor 2.5, density 3000 kg/m3. DV: thickness_cp (3 control points, 0.01 to 0.5 m). Constraints: structural failure <= 0, thickness does not intersect airfoil.
